violations.text: At the time of inspection, Milk, Milk Products, and Apple Cider were observed held at 43-53 degrees Fahrenheit in the countertop cooler unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. At the time of inspection, items observed at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit were moved to colder refrigeration, and items observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit or higher were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed to be in need of adjustment/repair... - The countertop cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit (the back of the unit was observed to be covered with a thick layer of ice). Unit was taken out of use and set for thawing at the time of inspection. - The faucet to the 3 bay sink was observed to be leaking Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Eggs were observed held at 105 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC re-heated eggs at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2024

The establishment was observed allowing a dog within the facility without asking/confirming, if it was a service animal and what service did it provide. The definition for "service animal" is adapted from 28 CFR 36.104 adopted pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) of 1990 (42 U.S.C. 12101 et seq.). A service animal performs some of the functions that persons with a disability cannot perform for themselves, such as those provided by "seeing eye dogs"; alerting persons with hearing impairments to sounds; pulling wheelchairs or carrying and picking up things for persons with mobility impairments; and assisting persons with mobility impairments with balance. A service animal is not considered to be a pet. New Violation. Correct By: 07/03/2024

At the time of inspection, an on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. At the time of of arrival for inspection, the owner Licensed CFSM was not on site. Owner did arrive by the end of the inspection. Owner stated employees are currently taking/preparing for a food safety course test. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2023
